rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN VS. IGP INDUSTRIES, LLC, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Seal Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 2, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT IGP INDUSTRIES, LLC, Motion to Seal Records. Motion to seal granted. An overriding interest overcomes the right of public access, the overriding interest supports sealing, a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed, the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. [CRC 2.550(d)] Exhibit B was communicated to an attorney for advice and is attorney-client privilege. Exhibits E and F contain confidential, non-public financial information of former and current employees. The excerpts refer to the contents of Exhibit B. The proposed order granting the motion to seal must include the language from this tentative decision. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W THAYER VS. BENJAMIN FRANKLIN PLUMBING et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 2, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT BENJAMIN FRANKLIN PLUMBING, and, DAN GAGNE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend to allow plaintiff to establish and plead standing. No statutory authority or case law supports contractual rights as community property. The causes of action are not time-barred. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR FADAYEL VS. MARIA FLORES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Seal Records And Register Of Actions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 2, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT MARIA FLORES Motion To Seal Records and Register Of Actions. Is granted. Overriding interest supports sealing records. Substaintial probability exists that defendant's interest will be prejudicial if record is not sealed. CRC 2.550, 2.551. =(302/CWW)
